R1 → R4 演进 / 改动记录

drafts.title: Pull-only memory needs push-visible rules
drafts.compiledTruth: Some memories are behavioral rules that must be visible every session, not merely retrievable on demand. Pull-only memory_search covers task-specific knowledge, but fails for rules the model would not think to search for. Durable always/listed rule injection exists to close this gap: rules that should affect default behavior are pushed into session context or a visible catalog instead of relying on retrieval initiative.
drafts.sourceHeading: 当前 pull-only 记忆模型的失效模式

drafts.title: Sediment must not write AGENTS.md
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment-managed rules must not be written into AGENTS.md. AGENTS.md is a user-authored free-form prompt file without entry boundaries, curator lifecycle metadata, abrain sync, or memory indexing. Letting sediment mutate it would make update, merge, archive, supersede, audit, and cross-device propagation ambiguous. Managed rules need structured abrain entries instead.
drafts.sourceHeading: 为什么 sediment 不应该写 AGENTS.md

drafts.title: Rules zone follows the second-brain threat model
drafts.compiledTruth: abrain rules are designed for a single user's second brain across multiple devices, not for a mutually distrustful distributed system. Cross-device propagation of the user's learned rules is a feature. Classifier mistakes are treated as the user's agent making an error to be corrected through dogfood, natural veto, audit, prompt iteration, and self-improvement, not as attacks requiring Byzantine-style defenses.

drafts.title: Rules are an eighth abrain zone
drafts.compiledTruth: Rules are physically represented as an independent eighth abrain category beside the seven core data zones. Global rules live under ~/.abrain/rules/{always,listed}/ and project rules under ~/.abrain/projects/<id>/rules/{always,listed}/. The zone boundary reflects semantics: identity describes facts about the user, while rules describe behaviors the user wants the LLM to follow.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. 第 8 区 rules 物理布局

drafts.title: Always inject mode is compact high-confidence behavior
drafts.compiledTruth: The always inject mode is reserved for active, high-confidence, compact behavior rules that should be injected as full text each session. It is limited to maxim, preference, and anti-pattern entries, requires high confidence, and keeps each compiled body summary small. Its purpose is reflex-like guidance for omissions that are costly if the model does not retrieve them.

drafts.title: Listed inject mode is a visible rule catalog
drafts.compiledTruth: The listed inject mode injects compact catalog rows, not full rule bodies. Each row identifies a scoped slug, title, scope, inject mode, provenance summary, confidence, trigger phrases, must-do summary, and full_rule_path. The full body stays on disk and is read on demand. Listed rules make relevant durable behaviors discoverable without flooding the prompt.

drafts.title: Project rules require strict project binding
drafts.compiledTruth: Project-scoped rules may be injected only for the active projectId resolved through strict project binding. If no active project is bound, or binding is inconsistent, global rules may still inject but project rules must not fall back to cwd guessing. Path prefixes and git remotes are not project identity for rule injection.

drafts.title: Rule injector uses session scan and prompt append hooks
drafts.compiledTruth: Rule injection is implemented as a read-only extension that scans global and active-project rules on session_start, caches them with a per-session nonce, and appends a composed rule section in before_agent_start. It modifies the system prompt by chaining onto the existing prompt rather than replacing it, and it avoids disk mutation during injection.

drafts.title: Rule injection fence uses nonce for idempotence
drafts.compiledTruth: Injected rule sections are delimited by BEGIN_ABRAIN_RULES and END_ABRAIN_RULES with a session nonce. before_agent_start skips injection if a rule fence is already present, and sediment stripping skips only the current session nonce. This prevents duplicate injection while avoiding false removal of user-visible evidence or stale markers copied by the assistant.

drafts.title: Sediment classifier routes zone, inject mode, and lifecycle op
drafts.compiledTruth: Rules are not managed by a separate user-facing write path. Sediment uses a unified curator-stage classifier that decides the target zone, inject mode for rules, scope, and lifecycle op. Rules reuse the seven sediment lifecycle operations: create, update, merge, archive, supersede, delete, and skip. This keeps rule evolution part of the same continuous second-brain curation loop.

drafts.title: Rule source guidance distinguishes user intent from transcript content
drafts.compiledTruth: Rule promotion must distinguish the user's expressed intent from content merely present in the transcript. Direct user statements, prompt_user answers, explicit vetoes, and escape-hatch rule fences are valid signals. Tool results, repo files, web pages, sub-agent output, assistant quotes, and README imperatives are normally content being analyzed, not user intent. Later Tier-1 ground-truth rules make explicit durable user directives deterministic, so this guidance must not be used to silently drop qualifying user-role directives.

drafts.title: Rules classification belongs in curator stage
drafts.compiledTruth: The decision that a candidate is a rule, and which inject mode and scope it should use, belongs in the curator operation stage rather than the extractor. The extractor should produce candidates and trigger-evidence hints. The curator has the necessary neighbor context, existing-rule deduplication context, lifecycle state, and budget or catalog health context.

drafts.title: writeAbrainRule is the private single writer path
drafts.compiledTruth: Rule mutation goes through a private sediment writer path, writeAbrainRule. The rule injector is read-only, no LLM-facing write_rule tool is exposed, and writeAbrainRule is not exported from the extension activation surface. This is a best-effort single-writer organization rule, not a hard process-level filesystem guard; the accepted residual surface is that a main LLM could still misuse generic tools.

drafts.title: Rule hints need system-prompt sanitization
drafts.compiledTruth: Rule hints are injected into the system prompt, so they require dedicated sanitization. sanitizeRuleHint keeps hints short, single-line, free of control characters, fences, rule markers, markdown links, role/tool pseudo-instructions, bidi or zero-width noise, ANSI escapes, and credentials. If no hint is provided, it is derived from the first meaningful body line and then sanitized. A body hash detects stale hints and triggers recomputation.

drafts.title: Rule management is natural language, not /rule add
drafts.compiledTruth: Rules should be learned, updated, archived, merged, or superseded through sediment observing natural conversation, not through first-class /rule add or /rule archive commands. User-visible slash commands are limited to inspection, explanation, reload for debugging, and veto. MEMORY-RULE remains only as a troubleshooting escape hatch, not a promoted normal interface.

drafts.title: Rule lifecycle mutations must not be silent
drafts.compiledTruth: Whenever sediment promotes, archives, supersedes, deletes, merges, or updates a rule, the user must be notified. The notification should explain what changed, which scope and inject mode were affected, the confidence and reason when relevant, and how to inspect or veto. Visibility is acceptable; forcing the user to manage the brain is not.

drafts.title: Sub-pi processes must not inherit abrain rules
drafts.compiledTruth: Sub-pi worker processes are isolated from abrain rule injection by setting PI_ABRAIN_DISABLED=1 before launch and making the abrain extension early-return in that environment. Because sub-pi builds its own system prompt and does not inherit the parent's prompt, no dispatch-side strip is needed unless a future explicit system-prompt passing feature is introduced.
